messages.author: Meno
messages.text: Hello everyone Im just curious about one thing. How its a off-grid project and the software flasher dont have even a offline ui to make it easy.
messages.content_html: Hello everyone Im just curious about one thing. How its a off-grid project and the software flasher dont have even a offline ui to make it easy.

messages.author: samfromnz
messages.text: Depending on your device, you can put it in DFU mode and plug it into a computer. Then it’s a simple as dragging and dropping the firmware file to your device and waiting for it to reboot.
messages.content_html: Depending on your device, you can put it in DFU mode and plug it into a computer. Then it’s a simple as dragging and dropping the firmware file to your device and waiting for it to reboot.
messages.reply_html:
Meno
Hello everyone Im just curious about one thing. How its a off-grid project and the software flasher dont have even a offline ui to make it easy.

messages.author: ViezeVingertjes
messages.text: The flasher is just a UI to make it easier, but you can always just compile and flash locally through platformio.
messages.content_html: The flasher is just a UI to make it easier, but you can always just compile and flash locally through platformio.

messages.author: Meno
messages.text: Thanks for the answers But i mean an .exe for windos so to be easy to flash any new device. But if i download the firmware and i insert in the new device is it will be simple to install it? Sorry about my stupid questions but im totaly new in this kind of thing's

messages.author: recrof
messages.text: backend that gets info from github and compiles the list of up to date firmwares is closed source, and will not be published. there are 2 cli utilities that you can use to flash nrf52 and esp32 nodes: nrf52: adafruit-nrfutil: https://github.com/adafruit/Adafruit_nRF52_nrfutil esp32: esptool: https://github.com/espressif/esptool
messages.content_html: backend that gets info from github and compiles the list of up to date firmwares is closed source, and will not be published. there are 2 cli utilities that you can use to flash nrf52 and esp32 nodes: nrf52: adafruit-nrfutil: https://github.com/adafruit/Adafruit_nRF52_nrfutil esp32: esptool: https://github.com/espressif/esptool
messages.reply_html:
Cisien
@recrof would need to provide details on how it all is setup since its not documented

messages.author: Clinton
messages.text: I could see a case where you have extra repeaters on hand but don't bother flashing them until you need them. Or where you have a bunch of repeaters but they aren't on the latest version. It would be handy to be able to have the files beforehand but not flash them until you need them.
messages.content_html: I could see a case where you have extra repeaters on hand but don't bother flashing them until you need them. Or where you have a bunch of repeaters but they aren't on the latest version. It would be handy to be able to have the files beforehand but not flash them until you need them.
messages.reply_html:
Cisien
If you are flashing in a “grid down” scenario where you cant access the internet then you already lost. You should have everything flashed and tested before shit and fan meet. You should also practice flashing via the cli tools

messages.author: bp-gone
messages.text: Well you are going to flash them to test that they work. So you will have at the very least an older version on them. If you are really prepping for off the grid action, you should be keeping copies of the latest firmware for your nodes as they are released. Offline flashing of nRF nodes is trivial as it’s drag and drop.
messages.content_html: Well you are going to flash them to test that they work. So you will have at the very least an older version on them. If you are really prepping for off the grid action, you should be keeping copies of the latest firmware for your nodes as they are released. Offline flashing of nRF nodes is trivial as it’s drag and drop.
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: ViezeVingertjes
messages.text: Also its simply not needed; you could be running a months old version and it would still work. Wouldnt bother if such scenario arises where you depend on it to update anything, till then, update every now and then to get the improvements.
messages.content_html: Also its simply not needed; you could be running a months old version and it would still work. Wouldnt bother if such scenario arises where you depend on it to update anything, till then, update every now and then to get the improvements.
